Docs are no longer just for humans.

They are becoming the interface for coding agents.

A good README should explain how to run, where key files live, what patterns to follow, how to test, and what “done” means.

Agent-readable repos will ship faster.
Observing your own code is becoming even more important with AI-generated changes. If review gets faster but observability stays weak, teams just discover mistakes later and with less context.

Context management + generated code review is the right workshop focus. The teams that get value from AI coding usually treat it as a workflow redesign problem, not a 'install tool, ship faster' problem.

record.text: Before anyone leaps on me, reading this as justification for using AI to write for you - it's not, I don't believe that, I don't think you *can* have something write *for* you. You asked it to produce a vaguely plausible text based on learning plagiarized from unpaid authors who worked hard.

It's an easy dunk to criticise writers who resort to LLMs to write their stories or nonfiction for them as lazy & talentless, but it exists against a background of collapsing arts funding, gutted arts & humanities depts in higher education, & higher living costs that make writing harder & harder.
Mine, I'm sorry to say, is 'like'. I go full Shaggy from Scooby Doo in any improvised public speaking scenario.

external.description: Async AI agents are not just chatbots with tools. Once agents run long-running tasks, call external systems, edit files, retry failed steps, and produce durable outputs, they become distributed systems with a model at the center. The reliability of these agents depends less on the model alone and more on the infrastructure around it: queues, state stores, controlled tool execution, artifacts, observability, permissions, and recovery paths.

record.text: (par incidence sur les votes pour le parti originel, j’entends : contrer l’AfD/RN)

Exactly — and Hermes makes each option explicit. /goal: judge model. Kanban: external state (SQLite) + human review gates.

What we run (Aïda/OpenClaw-style): LLM decides. The failure mode: drift. The model says done. Then keeps going. Hard to audit, hard to halt.
Full article: https://schrijfhuis.jongbloed.net/hermes-why-does-an-ai-tool-need-two-goal-engines/

Hermes calls itself a 'runtime.' Not an assistant. Not a copilot.

The chat interface is a thin frontend. You can stop using Telegram tomorrow — the runtime is still there.

Most AI tools collapse the execution layer into the user-facing layer. Hermes exposes it.

沖縄の戦争時避難計画に抗議の声が多いの、攻撃理由である米軍基地の存在に対して、内地への分散をろくにやらず、努力もお座なりなのに、現地住民に大きな負担と不安が与えられる政策には力こぶ作ってどんどん進めるってやり方で、その上「お前らのための安全措置に非協力的とか頭おかしいよね」まで言われたら、頭こないほうがおかしいだろ。

中国への距離が近いのも米軍基地が集積してるのも沖縄の特性で、政府の責任ではなく一種の運命なんだから沖縄県民は甘受すべきだし、嫌なら出ていけばｗ　みたいな話なんだよな。

治安の悪いSNSで権力批判すると攻撃的なリプライが殺到して鍵垢にせざるを得なくなる構図、ブログ全盛の頃にもコメントスクラムが頻発してたのと一緒なのよな。

そのコメントスクラムの時代に「ネット右翼」という言葉が言われ始めて、そのネット右翼たちに人気絶大だったのがJSF氏だった。

まあ、検索して出てくるサイトが、何も知らない馬鹿がAI使って賢くなった気になって作ってる代物ばかりになってきて、ロボット検索自体の死が近づいてる感はあるけど。

一度滅んだディレクトリ型サービス、はてなあたりが復活させんかな。

images.alt:
📜 Beschreibung 》
Einst war Sapphire die Prinzessin von Silverland, doch von ihrer Heimat blieb nach der Katastrophe Nergal nur Verlust und Schmerz zurück. Auf ihrer ziellosen Reise gelangt sie nach Goldland, wo sie zum ersten Mal wieder Hoffnung spürt. Die Freundlichkeit der Menschen lässt sie glauben, dass ein neues Leben möglich sein könnte. Doch als Nergal auch dieses Land bedroht, stellt sich Sapphire ihrem Schicksal. Dieses Mal will sie nicht fliehen, sondern kämpfen.
